This is big, team: the Washington Post is reporting that John McCain says he'll support a new GOP Obamacare repeal bill.

**[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d839/2b07114c/186a5cd8/73134446/1961860261/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/save-obamacare?source=PT-20170907-Breaking_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")**
_**The Washington Post
"McCain considers a new Obamacare repeal bill"**_
This is big, team: the _Washington Post_ is reporting that John McCain just announced he's willing to support a new GOP Obamacare repeal bill introduced by his good friend Lindsey Graham.

McCain was the deciding vote against Obamacare repeal when it came to the Senate floor in July. If this new bill makes it that far, a flip-flop vote from McCain could literally mean the end of Obamacare.

[**We have to stop this new repeal bill before it gets any further. Add your name right now to show Republicans we 'll hold them accountable if they rip away coverage from millions of Americans.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d839/2b07114c/186a5cd8/73134446/1961860261/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/save-obamacare?source=PT-20170907-Breaking_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

**Senate Republicans have until September 30 to pass an Obamacare repeal bill with just 50 votes before the House version expires.**

That means that McConnell is desperate to force through a new Obamacare repeal bill this month -- and McCain's announcement could be just what he needs to get it done.

We can't let our guard down on Obamacare repeal until the September 30 deadline -- not even for a minute and definitely not now that McCain is switching sides.

**Team, speak up right now to show Republicans the consequences they 'll face for trying again to repeal Obamacare. Add your name:**

Team DSCC

subject: third most vulnerable senator
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|
---

| | | **CNN: Indiana Senate Seat _Third Most Likely_
to Flip in 2018
[Pitch in to keep Joe in the Senate >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c278c/7377f838/37d3f0bc/51fbfd3a/1851661933/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-cnn?refcode=20170907nd&amount=10")**
---
Peter -- CNN named Joe the **_third most_ vulnerable senator up for re-election this cycle**. Republicans are already pouring money into Indiana to attack Joe, and this news will grab the attention of even more GOP mega donors.

**Look, nobody works harder for Hoosiers than Joe.** With your support, he played a crucial role in blocking Trumpcare, defended our public education from budget cuts, advocated for our servicemembers and veterans, and fought to fund programs to get Hoosiers back to work.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Our moral obligation to act on climate change
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
The devastation of Harvey is a terrible tragedy -- but it should not be a surprise.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTE4NzE2NDAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MDYwNzAzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, Harvey's impact on the Houston area has been tragic, devastating, and dramatic. One thing it is not, however, is surprising. Indeed, scientists have been warning us for many years that climate change would lead to more intense precipitation, worse flooding than ever before, and unpredictable, massive storms. So while an individual weather event is not proof of global climate change, a pattern of more frequent devastating natural disasters is. And we know that storms like Harvey will become yet more destructive. Our seas are rising. Our atmosphere holds more moisture. And the impact of climate change is being felt by people right now, from Southeast Asia to Houston. And now Irma is threatening the people of Florida. In Washington, our forests are burning, our cities are clouded over with smoke, and ash is falling in Seattle instead of rain. So while officials at every level are rightfully focused on helping the people of Texas recover, it must also be our duty to continue the long, hard work of combating global climate change. It's why my state of Washington, and many places across America, will continue to abide by the terms of the Paris Agreement. And it's why we'll continue to grow our clean energy economy as a regional and global leader. [**Join me, right now, and call on all leaders on both sides of the aisle to take the threat of climate change seriously -- starting by reengaging with the Paris Agreement.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTE4NzE2NDAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MDYwNzAzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0) If we deny climate science, we're gambling with our lives and the lives of our children. We should heed the warnings and come together as a nation to deal with this crisis. Acknowledging scientific fact is one critical step towards preparing our communities for weather events like Harvey and Irma in the future. Denying climate science also shortchanges our economy. In Washington, we've led a regional and global effort to grow the clean energy economy -- creating jobs here at home. It's not only the right thing to do, it's the smart thing to do. We can no longer be surprised by the extreme weather that scientists have been predicting for well over a decade. We can no longer pretend that climate change is a challenge for another generation. It's not only our moral obligation to act -- action is essential to preserving our children's quality of life. [**Add your name today to stand with climate science.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTE4NzE2NDAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MDYwNzAzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0) I hope you'll join me in the fight. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTE4NzE2NDAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MDYwNzAzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---

name: Samantha Gross (via vanhollen.org)
subject: protecting DREAMers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| Did you see Chris' email yesterday regarding the Trump administration's decision to rescind DACA?

[**Sign here to stand with Chris and demand the Senate take immediate action to protect 800,000 DREAMers and preserve DACA. Read Chris' email below >> **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd3/70cdc2ba/469c6405/397dd2c/918876262/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/defend-dreamers?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Sign+here+to+stand+with+Chris+and+demand&utm_campaign=em090717&source=em090717")

_Begin forwarded message:_

> The Trump administration's decision to end the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program puts the lives of 800,000 young people (known as DREAMers) into limbo and worse.
>
> These individuals came to our country as infants and young children when they were too young to have a say. They have done nothing wrong. In communities across America, they are attending school, holding productive jobs and contributing to our country and our economy.
>
> [**As the New York Times said this morning: "DACA is morally right, legally sound and fiscally smart policy". I agree. That's why I hope you will join me in demanding an immediate vote in the Senate to take action to save DACA >> **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd3/70cdc2ba/469c6405/397dd2d/918876262/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/defend-dreamers?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+As+the+New+York+Times+said+this+morning+&utm_campaign=em090717&source=em090717")
>
> It's clear that disrupting the lives of DREAMers is not only against American values; it's a disaster for our economy. If DACA recipients were to lose their work permits and jobs, it would cost the U.S. economy over $400 billion over 10 years -- $400 million over one year in Maryland alone.
>
> We all want safe and secure borders, but deporting Dreamers is not the way to achieve that goal.
>
> [**10,000 Maryland DREAMers know only one home --the United States of America. It's time we fight like hell for them and demand that Mitch McConnell immediately bring the bi-partisan Dreamers Bill up for a vote in the Senate. Are you with me?**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd3/70cdc2ba/469c6405/397dd2a/918876262/VEsF/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/defend-dreamers?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+10000+Maryland+DREAMers+know+only+one+ho&utm_campaign=em090717&source=em090717")
>
> Thank you for all you do,
>
> Chris Van Hollen
> U.S. Senator

---

---

Peter,

EMILY's List is all about early money.

(It's _literally_ in our name: Early Money Is Like Yeast... it makes the dough rise!)

Here's how it works: The political gatekeepers often want to see that a candidate can raise money _before_ getting behind them. So we raise early money for candidates, proving they are viable, which in turn encourages others to invest in them.

We're supporting **47** candidates running for office right now, and more than half of them are running to flip red seats blue. By showing our support for them today, we can have a huge impact on their races.

**[Donate $3 to help give our women running up and down the ballot the head start they need to win their races.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c67af/24c69fd9/a14d7b5a/189b21da/1581970461/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170907_email_WhyEarlyMoney?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+give+our+women+running+&utm_campaign=WQEFE911N_digital_email_20170907_WhyEarlyMoney_inact&source=WQEFE911N_digital_email_20170907_WhyEarlyMoney_inact")**

Friend,

This week, the Trump administration announced it is ending the DACA program.

That means the roughly 800,000 young children who were brought to this nation through no fault of their own are at risk of being deported from the only home they've ever known.

This is just wrong - ending DACA means we would lose nearly 27,000 hardworking members of our communities who made North Carolina their home, pay taxes, and contribute to our economy.

We made a promise to protect DACA recipients from deportation if they came forward. President Trump is playing politics with the lives of these people.

**I'm calling on Senator Burr, Senator Tillis and our House members to pass common sense immigration reform that will protect our borders, keep families together, and let these young people remain in the only place they've ever known as home.**
